commit.message:
Brighten text-muted and text-tertiary for dark theme readability

In all three dark themes, text-muted and text-tertiary were too dim
to read against the dark sidebar and surface backgrounds. Bumped:

Dark/Aubergine:
  text-tertiary: #9ca3af → #b0b8c4 (contrast ~8:1 on sidebar)
  text-muted:    #6b7280 → #8b95a3 (contrast ~5.4:1 on sidebar)

Ocean:
  text-tertiary: #94a3b8 → #a8b8cc
  text-muted:    #64748b → #8494ab

This fixes visibility of sidebar section titles (Folders, Tags),
editor toolbar icons, and all other muted/tertiary text elements.

commit.message:
Implement BG3-style combat manager (3-screen system)

Replaces the basic Jugador/Enemigo combat mode with a full combat manager:

Screen 1 - Setup: Select participants from all available character sheets.
Each card shows portrait, class, HP and CA. Multi-select with count display.

Screen 2 - Initiative: Manual initiative input per participant (rolled with
physical dice). Sorted descending to build the turn order.

Screen 3 - Combat Manager (full-screen overlay):
- Top bar: Round badge + scrollable turn queue with HP bars, one card per participant
- Active panel: HP with +/-1/+/-5 buttons, CA, conditions bar, action chips
  grouped by Acciones/Adicionales/Reacciones (players), custom action input
- Recorded actions per turn with toggle (click to mark/unmark)
- Combat log: all turns newest-first, each editable with ✏️ to add/remove
  actions or custom text after the fact
- Next Turn advances and creates new log entry; round counter increments
  when wrapping back to first participant

Reuses: inferActionType, getDiceBadges, extractDiceFromDesc, CONDITIONS array
New: combatState global, full combat function suite (~450 lines)
Cache: bumped to v=20260308d

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: resolve ESLint circular config error and add CI environment variables

This fix addresses the CI/CD pipeline failures:

1. Fixed eslint.config.js circular structure error by replacing FlatCompat
   wrapper with proper ESLint flat config format using direct plugin imports
   from @next/eslint-plugin-next and typescript-eslint

2. Added required environment variables to .github/workflows/test.yml:
   - NEXTAUTH_URL
   - NEXTAUTH_SECRET
   - BETTER_AUTH_SECRET

These changes ensure that:
- ESLint runs without crashing from circular reference errors
- TypeScript compilation succeeds
- GitHub Actions workflows have necessary environment variables

Resolves failing CI checks on GitHub.

commit.message:
feat: passive norm learning from group conversation patterns

Heuristic-based norm detection (zero LLM cost):
- Observes every message in a rolling 50-message buffer
- Every 30 min, infers norms: typing style, topic dominance,
  message length, emoji usage, question frequency
- Auto-records norms via existing addGroupNorm() infrastructure
- JARVIS adapts to each group's communication style over time

commit.message:
feat: implement DNA 4 — Governance & Harmony Records

Public DHT, open read / HTTP Gateway target. Two keys baked into DNA
properties gate all writes: harmony_record_creator_key (HarmonyRecord,
GovernanceDecision, ReproducibilityBadge) and system_coordinator_key
(ValidatorReputation). validate() enforces author == key for creates;
HarmonyRecord/GovernanceDecision/Badge are additionally immutable
(updates + deletes blocked).

Entry types: HarmonyRecord, ValidatorReputation, ReproducibilityBadge,
GovernanceDecision. Link types: ValidatorToReputation,
RequestToHarmonyRecord, DisciplinePath, BadgePath, StudyToBadge.

Coordinator:
- check_and_create_harmony_record: idempotent; calls attestation DNA
  for attestations, derives majority outcome + AgreementLevel, creates
  HarmonyRecord + discipline path index + optional badge
- update_validator_reputation: creates new ValidatorReputation entry
  (latest = last link), calculates tier from total + agreement_rate
- get_harmony_record, get_harmony_records_by_discipline,
  get_validator_reputation, get_badges_for_study (all unrestricted)
- init(): public-read cap grant for HTTP Gateway

happ.yaml: added researcher_repository, validator_workspace, governance
roles (all four DNAs now wired).

Tryorama tests (governance.test.ts):
1. Idempotency — two calls produce at most one HarmonyRecord
2. Author enforcement — non-creator key rejected by validate()
3. Full end-to-end — all 4 DNAs: study → request → private attestations
   → notify_commitment_sealed → RevealOpen → public attestations →
   HarmonyRecord on public DHT
4. ValidatorReputation author enforcement — non-coordinator rejected;
   coordinator key accepted

cargo check --target wasm32-unknown-unknown passes with 0 errors.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Implement TrafficSignLearningMode feature

- TrafficSignLearningMode enum: .assist / .learning
- TrafficSignMeaningOption struct: id, title, isCorrect
- TrafficSignHistoryEntry: add userSelectedMeaning, userAnswerCorrect (optional, backward compatible); two convenience inits (assist + learning)
- TrafficSignRecognitionService: add generateMeaningOptions(for:) returning 4 shuffled options (1 correct + 3 distractors from pool of 18 signs); reusable by TrafficSceneAnalyzer
- TrafficSignRecognitionViewModel: full learning mode state (currentMode, meaningOptions, selectedOptionId, userSubmitted, isCorrect); selectOption/submitAnswer; assist auto-saves immediately, learning saves after submit
- TrafficSignRecognitionView: mode picker (segmented Assist/Learning); Assist shows result card directly; Learning shows options with selection highlight + Submit button + result card (correct/incorrect header, user answer, correct answer, explanation, confidence bar)
- AnalysisDebugPanel: extended Last Sign section with mode label + learning fields (selected option, result)
- UX documentation updated for new feature flow

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix nvarchar conversion errors in lock parsing by using TRY_CAST (#147)

Replace CONVERT(INT, ...) with TRY_CAST(... AS INT) at 8 locations where
resource_description strings from sys.dm_tran_locks are parsed into integers.
SUBSTRING/CHARINDEX extraction can produce non-numeric values (spaces, parens)
from unexpected description formats, causing "Conversion failed when converting
the nvarchar value" errors. TRY_CAST returns NULL instead of throwing.

Using TRY_CAST rather than TRY_CONVERT for broader compatibility across
database compatibility levels.

Fixes #135

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* chore: remove single-version matrix so CI job is named 'test' not 'test (3.12)'

The branch protection required check was named 'test' but the matrix
strategy caused GitHub Actions to emit the check as 'test (3.12)',
meaning the required check never matched and the PR was permanently
blocked. Since we only test against Python 3.12 there is no need for
a matrix — removing it fixes the name and unblocks merge without --admin.

commit.message:
Migrate build tooling from Vite to tsdown (#46)

## Summary
This PR replaces the Vite build system with tsdown, a more lightweight
and specialized tool for building TypeScript/JavaScript libraries. The
migration simplifies the build configuration and removes unnecessary
build-related CI steps.

## Key Changes
- **Removed Vite configuration**: Deleted `vite.config.js` which defined
library build settings, entry points, and external dependencies
- **Added tsdown configuration**: Created `tsdown.config.js` with
equivalent build settings including ESM format, TypeScript definitions
generation, and publint validation
- **Updated build script**: Changed `package.json` build command from
`vite build` to `tsdown`
- **Removed lint-package CI job**: Eliminated the separate GitHub
Actions workflow step that ran `npm run build` followed by `publint`, as
tsdown now handles publint validation internally
- **Updated dependencies**: Removed `vite` and `vite-plugin-dts` dev
dependencies, added `tsdown` and `publint` as direct dev dependencies

## Notable Details
- The build output configuration remains functionally equivalent (ESM
format, same entry points)
- External dependency handling (`@projectwallace/css-analyzer`) is
preserved in the new configuration
- TypeScript definitions generation is maintained via tsdown's built-in
`dts` option
- Package linting is now integrated into the build process rather than
being a separate CI step

https://claude.ai/code/session_01VzmRVmcLD7znsBCH2V5Hg7

---------

Remove schwab-py, replace with direct httpx calls (#4)

* Remove SCHWAB_MCP_HOST/PORT env vars

Horizon controls bind address; schwab-py handles the Schwab API URL
internally. Hardcode defaults in __main__ for local dev only.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Remove schwab-py, replace with direct httpx calls

Replace the unofficial schwab-py wrapper with a thin SchwabClient class
that makes direct httpx calls to api.schwabapi.com. This eliminates the
hardcoded base URL issue, removes enum wrappers in favour of plain string
params, and gives full control over auth headers and token refresh.

- New schwab_client.py: async httpx client with bearer auth + proactive
  token refresh (300s leeway, asyncio.Lock for concurrent safety)
- Remove _RebasedAsyncClient hack from vault.py
- Tools now receive parsed dicts instead of response wrappers
- 48 tests pass (11 new for SchwabClient, simplified mocks elsewhere)
- schwab-py removed from pyproject.toml dependencies

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

GitHub integration (PAT or App auth) and agent credentials. The UI

via PubSub.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: add Tekla Structures 2025 support

- TeklaBridge now detects API version and applies version-specific IPC fix:
  TS2021: reflection patch of named-pipe channel names (-: → -Console:)
  TS2025: ApplyTs2025ChannelVersionFix reads installed FileVersion and patches fields
- Fixed DetectTeklaRootForMajor to accept both nt/bin (TS2021) and bin (TS2025) layouts
- Fixed XS_DIR and PATH env setup for TS2025 (no nt/ subdirectory)
- TeklaMcpServer.ResolveBridgePath() prefers extensions folder for TS2025+:
  C:\TeklaStructures\2025.0\Environments\common\extensions\svMCP\TeklaBridge.exe
- Updated NuGet packages to 2025.0.0 in TeklaBridge and TeklaMcpServer.Api
- Docs: README, ROADMAP, CLAUDE.md updated with TS2025 setup, IPC details, deploy instructions

Root cause of TS2025 failure: NuGet DLL FileVersion 2025.0.0.0 ≠ installed FileVersion 2025.0.52577.0.
MMF channel name embeds FileVersion, so NuGet client cannot find the installed Tekla MMF.
Solution: run TeklaBridge from extensions folder with exe.config <codeBase> entries pointing to
installed DLLs — CLR loads them with correct FileVersion, channel name matches.

because the new pod can't schedule while the old one holds the GPU.

commit.message:
Reduce seq lengths further: NER/Router 256, GQL 512

Model at 19.25GB + 512-token activations across 64 layers = 21.47GB,
exceeding L4's 22GB. At 256 tokens: ~20.35GB (fits). NER examples
are ~100-200 tokens, router even shorter. GQL needs more but 512
should suffice for most queries.

commit.message:
Classify UnpackedArrayConcatElaborates → §10.10 [skip ci]

The test assigns an unpacked array using the concatenation syntax `A = {1, 2, 3}`. This is the core unpacked array concatenation feature defined in §10.10. The subsections (10.10.1–10.10.3) cover comparisons and nesting, not the fundamental syntax and semantics being exercised here.

commit.message:
fix: create home_membership on intake + warn admin about placeholder emails

Two critical issues:
1. Intake "new home" path created a home with owner_id but never created a
   home_memberships record. The family dashboard's /my-homes endpoint only
   checks memberships, so clients saw an empty dashboard with no home.
2. When a duplicate email falls back to @placeholder.rockwell, the welcome
   email goes nowhere. Now surfaces a warning on the confirmation page so
   the admin knows to update the email or manually set the password.
